description: Whitelist of shell commands that let Evaluator SubAgents use MCP-backed checks without direct MCP tool access.

source_model.trae_ui_mcp: TRAE UI-registered MCP servers are available to the root agent, but are not assumed to be visible from the remote environment shell or SubAgents.
source_model.mcporter_discovery: Reads /workspace/config/mcporter.json (mcporter standard config). Discovery runs `mcporter list` at install time, output saved to harness/mcp-bridge/discovery/mcporter-list.txt. Adding a new MCP server only requires editing config/mcporter.json.
source_model.bridge_architecture: Open-closed principle: config/mcporter.json is the single source of MCP server declarations. The wrapper (mcp-browser) is a generic thin shell that only validates whitelist + forwards to `mcporter call server.tool`. Adding a new MCP server = edit config/mcporter.json + append to wrapper ALLOWED array. No changes to install.sh/check.sh core logic.
source_model.subagent_runtime: Evaluator SubAgents may only call wrapper commands listed below, not raw MCP tools or undiscovered mcporter calls.

commands.name: mcp-browser
commands.purpose: Browser/UI evidence collection from Evaluator context.

forbidden: Do not expose arbitrary MCP tools.
Do not use bridge commands to mutate production state.
Do not let roles call commands not listed in this manifest.
fallback: If check.sh reports unavailable, use orchestrator_delegated mode or stop with [BLOCKED: MCP bridge unavailable].
